_status: SKELETON DRAFT 2026-05-25. One of six Ch2 dependencies blocking court_rounds/chapter2_round_1.json. Code-pass only; every display field stubbed DESIGN_TODO per the Ch1 author convention. STRUCTURAL DEPARTURE from argument_frames_ch1.json: Ch2 Round 1 does NOT use motion-packet completeness — it uses Phase 1 fact-flags (see court_rounds/chapter2_round_1.json::phase_1_fact_finding). The packet_requirements and consumes_packet_completeness keys are therefore omitted; phase_1_fact_requirements replaces them. Confirm shape with Design before authoring Round 2 / Round 3 entries: if Ch2 keeps a separate packet-completeness pass for the meta-round, the omitted keys come back.
_doc: Chapter 2 frame/blunder selection. Round 1 entries only; Round 2 (likely substantive-rights frame set) and Round 3 (likely remedy-discipline / proportionality frame set) deferred until the corresponding court_rounds files are authored.
_owner_code: id, kind, supporting_evidence, statute_anchor, source_npc_primary, strength_rating, well_fitted, court_round_unlock, requires_flags, phase_1_fact_requirements, judge_reaction_template, judicial_patience_delta_on_select, halina_trust_delta_on_select, burns_round_attempt
_open_design_questions: Q1: do Ch2 frames carry halina_trust_delta_on_select at all? Halina trust is a Ch1 mechanic per the trust-meter rework deferred in 2026-05-19 narrative remediation. Current: deltas all 0 placeholder; remove the key entirely if trust is sunset post-Ch1.

Q2: does Ch2 use court_round_unlock the same way (round_1_open / round_2_open / round_3_remedy) or do we need ch2-specific unlock ids? Current: uses ch2_round_1_open / ch2_round_2_open / ch2_round_3_open as a guess; reconcile with battle_controller.gd::_unlock_round once Code reviews.

Q3: should phase_1_fact_requirements duplicate the requires_fact_flags already in chapter2_round_1.json::frame_gates.<id>.available_counter_questions[].requires_fact_flags, or only document the floor that distinguishes frame from blunder? Current: documents the floor; the per-counter-question gates remain authoritative in the court-rounds file.

phase_1_fact_inputs._evaluation_note: Frame/blunder evaluation reads Phase 1 fact-flag count first, then applies the per-entry phase_1_fact_requirements floor below.

rule_vagueness.statute_anchor: PL Const Art 75 (proper housing) + Convention Art 8 — clarity of law

phase_1_fact_requirements._status_note: Floor for the frame to be a legitimate choice rather than a blunder. The judge's jq_dignity_definition gate requires the additional _fact.no_published_examples flag, which is enforced inside chapter2_round_1.json, not duplicated here.

overbreadth.statute_anchor: PL Const Art 32 (equal treatment) + Convention Art 8 — overbroad sweep of expressive conduct

phase_1_fact_requirements._status_note: Floor: poster_unique_target establishes the overbreadth angle (the rule's scope sweeps in protected expression that the building has never enforced against). jq_scope counter-question requires only this flag; jq_targeted_enforcement additionally requires _fact.no_published_examples.

arbitrary_enforcement.statute_anchor: PL Const Art 32 — uniform application of law

phase_1_fact_requirements._status_note: Floor: BOTH flags required. arbitrary_enforcement is the strongest of the three frames on the merits but the most fact-expensive — surfacing _fact.no_published_examples requires the administrator_beton.rule_history press path, which competes with the rule_text definitional pressure on the same witness's cooperation_budget.

merits_defence.statute_anchor: Substantive housing-law merits — out of place at the rule-clarity stage

phase_1_fact_requirements._status_note: Blunder path: no fact-flag floor. Selecting merits_defence in Round 1 burns the round attempt and starts Phase 2 with judicial_patience: 3 per chapter2_round_1.json::frame_gates.merits_defence. Mirrors the Ch1 substantive_defense blunder shape.
